title: Gelrebia dauensis

The plant's classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Saxifragales, Family: Crassulaceae, Genus: Gelrebia, Species: G. dauensis. intro: Gelrebia dauensis is a medicinal plant species, commonly known as Gelrebia, that belongs to the family Crassulaceae. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Gelrebia dauensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Gelrebia dauensis include teas, tinctures, and ointments, which are used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds, as well as taken orally to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation.
